judge_reason: Description A claims the envelope is addressed to 'Mr Dominic Gerardi' while Description B reads 'Mr Pomonic Gerardi', contradicting the specific quoted text of the recipient's name.
original_image_tags: Black-and-white photograph of a typewritten envelope against a dark background. The envelope is addressed to "Mr Dominic Gerardi" and has a return address from "Director of Operations, Electronics Service Unit 4". A postmark is visible in the upper right. Handwritten annotations are present on the envelope and at the bottom of the photograph.
A 3-cent U.S. Postage stamp featuring the Statue of Liberty. The word "LIBERTY" is visible below the statue.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Black-and-white photograph of a sealed envelope addressed to “Mr. Somonic Gerardi, Torrington, Connecticut,” postmarked Roseau, Minn. Jul 21 7:30 AM 1958, with a U.S. 3-cent postage stamp and handwritten “183 Hamilton Ave” on the front.*
